metadata.author: LivingPatch Contributors
metadata.description: Eastern Kingbird species with ecological relationships and behavioral characteristics

species.functional_description: Medium-sized flycatcher (8-9 inches) with distinctive black and white plumage and concealed red crown patch (rarely visible). Aggressive and highly territorial — known for chasing larger birds including hawks and crows from their territory. Expert aerial insectivore constantly sallying out to catch insects in flight. Nests in open branches where nests are highly visible. Harsh, buzzing calls are characteristic of open field habitat.

life_stages.name: Territorial Arrival
life_stages.description: Males arrive before females to establish and defend breeding territory

life_stages.name: Nest Building
life_stages.description: Pairs build cup nests on open branches in trees or shrubs

life_stages.name: Egg
life_stages.description: Clutch of 3-5 whitish eggs with dark spots

life_stages.name: Chick
life_stages.description: Nestlings in open nest; parents make continuous insect-catching flights

life_stages.name: Fledgling
life_stages.description: Young kingbirds still being fed by parents; learning to catch insects

life_stages.name: Pre-migration
life_stages.description: Feeding intensively before southbound migration to Central/South America
